methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $32M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Planted Solar is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Eric Brown that has raised $32M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable in the Climate & Energy sector, including direct stage peers Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the $32M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applies a typical 2.0x Series A step-up multiplier, implying a $64M valuation. The peer-median method benchmarks against three same-stage, same-sector comparables with a median valuation of $150M and a range of $110M–$200M. This method directly reflects market pricing for comparable companies at identical development stages.

Triangulation of these two methods yields a median valuation of $107M, with a low-end estimate of $54M and a high-end estimate of $172M. The gap between methods reflects material uncertainty around whether Planted Solar should command a premium or discount relative to the peer median. Key variables affecting positioning within this range include competitive differentiation relative to Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy; capital efficiency relative to the $32M raised; and near-term revenue traction or customer commitments. The $107M median represents roughly 3.3x total capital raised.
